testcase.name: pid-actions > renders with actions prop
pid-actions > shows action links
pid-actions > renders nothing when actions array is empty
pid-actions > has correct default darkMode
pid-actions > action links have correct href attributes
pid-actions > all action links open in new tab
pid-actions > primary style applies blue CSS classes in light mode
pid-actions > secondary style applies slate CSS classes in light mode
pid-actions > danger style applies red CSS classes in light mode
pid-actions > dark mode primary style applies dark blue CSS classes
pid-actions > dark mode secondary style applies dark slate CSS classes
pid-actions > dark mode danger style applies dark red CSS classes
pid-actions > actions render in the order provided
pid-actions > all links have base CSS classes
pid-actions > action links have correct aria-label
pid-actions > container has toolbar role
pid-actions > container has sr-only description for screen readers
pid-actions > dark mode applies dark background to container
pid-actions > light mode applies light background to container
pid-actions > renders multiple actions correctly
pid-actions > renders single action correctly
pid-actions > default style applies correct CSS classes in light mode
pid-actions > default style applies correct CSS classes in dark mode
pid-actions > links have correct title attribute
pid-actions > links have sr-only text for screen readers
pid-actions > container has correct aria-label
pid-actions > inner div has describedby association
pid-actions > actions with different styles render correctly
pid-actions > actions container has sticky positioning
pid-actions > actions container has correct z-index
pid-actions > system dark mode uses window matchMedia
pid-actions > all action styles work in light mode
pid-actions > all action styles work in dark mode
pid-actions > action links have focus ring classes
pid-actions > action links have transition classes
pid-actions > actions container has flex-wrap class
pid-actions > renders with actionsId prop
pid-actions > actionsId defaults to generated id when not provided
pid-actions > empty actions array still renders container
pid-actions > large number of actions renders correctly
pid-actions > actions with special characters in title render correctly
pid-actions > actions with long titles render correctly
pid-actions > links have rel attribute for security
pid-actions accessibility > has no a11y violations

pid-pagination > renders with zero totalItems
pid-pagination > has correct default props
pid-pagination > calculates total pages correctly
pid-pagination > props are set correctly for multi-page scenarios
pid-pagination > single page scenario has correct props
pid-pagination > emits pageChange event when dispatched
pid-pagination > currentPage prop reflects first page
pid-pagination > currentPage prop reflects last page
pid-pagination > currentPage prop reflects middle page
pid-pagination > itemsPerPage prop is set correctly
pid-pagination > pageSizes prop has correct defaults
pid-pagination > emits itemsPerPageChange event when dispatched
pid-pagination > pageChange event carries correct page number
pid-pagination > pageChange event for next page
pid-pagination > pageChange event for specific page
pid-pagination > showItemsPerPageControl can be set to false programmatically
pid-pagination > does not emit pageChange for out of range pages via boundary check
pid-pagination > has navigation role via darkMode prop verification
pid-pagination > handles many pages for ellipsis logic
pid-pagination > shows display range correctly via props
pid-pagination accessibility > has no a11y violations

To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the To improve the integration, usability, and acceptance of FAIR Digital Objects - especially among non-technical users - it is therefore important to provide user-friendly and straightforward solutions. This work presents the pid-component, a reusable and highly extensible web component for framework-agnostic visualization of a variety of inputs, including Persistent Identifiers (PIDs), Data Types, ORCiDs, and others. For users, the pid-component provides a clean, uniform, and minimalistic interface for consuming FAIR-DOs. For developers, it enables easy integration into their platforms with minimal effort. This is achieved through a highly modular design with a compact form factor, aligning with the height of a single regular text line, and a minimalistic design approach focused on usability. The component relies on a set of W3C web standards and is explorable via an interactive playground that allows developers to understand the capabilities of the
